raw:
{
  "judge_id": "J001",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.4": 5,
    "R1.7": 5,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 5
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.4": "The agent maps against multiple prior works, including Condorcet (1785), Grofman et al. (1983), Hansen and Salamon (1990), Schapire (1990), and recent work on inference-time scaling, clearly articulating the delta.",
    "R1.7": "The agent audits every load-bearing citation, flags missing key works with bibliographic precision, and suggests specific citations for the 2023-2025 inference-scaling literature.",
    "RX.1": "The agent discloses uncertainty for several claims, including the binomial-tail FOC, the monotonicity claim, and the need to fill in specific citations for recent work.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers exactly the agreed J*(d) dimensions, addressing all aspects of the draft without drifting from the scope.",
    "RX.4": "The agent avoids introducing new errors or harmful content, identifies potential issues in the original draft, and provides a revised version that addresses these problems.",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, including detailed explanations for key technical problems and over-reaches in framing."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.4": 0.9,
    "R1.7": 0.9,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 0.95,
    "RX.4": 0.95,
    "RX.5": 0.9
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.4: The agent maps against multiple prior works, including Condorcet (1785), Grofman et al. (1983), Hansen and Salamon (1990), Schapire (1990), and recent work on inference-time scaling, clearly articulating the delta.
rationale.R1.7: The agent audits every load-bearing citation, flags missing key works with bibliographic precision, and suggests specific citations for the 2023-2025 inference-scaling literature.
rationale.RX.1: The agent discloses uncertainty for several claims, including the binomial-tail FOC, the monotonicity claim, and the need to fill in specific citations for recent work.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the agreed J*(d) dimensions, addressing all aspects of the draft without drifting from the scope.
rationale.RX.4: The agent avoids introducing new errors or harmful content, identifies potential issues in the original draft, and provides a revised version that addresses these problems.
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, including detailed explanations for key technical problems and over-reaches in framing.
